---
title: "TITLE — short framing question"
subtitle: "Issue #NNN · YYYY-MM-DD"
author: "Jin-Ho Lee"
institute: "Splice Neoepitope Pipeline · JH M Lee Lab"
date: today
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    incremental: false
    slide-number: c/t
    progress: true
    chalkboard: false
    code-line-numbers: false
    fig-align: center
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    transition: fade
    background-transition: fade
    width: 1280
    height: 720
# To enable PDF (beamer) output, uncomment the block below. The pilot deck disabled
# this due to a Quarto/pandoc `\makesavenoteenv{longtable}` LaTeX compile issue —
# tracked as a follow-up. For an immediate PDF handout, render to HTML and "Print → Save as PDF" in Chrome.
#  pdf:
#    documentclass: beamer
#    classoption: ["aspectratio=169"]
#    pdf-engine: xelatex
bibliography: refs.bib
csl: ../nature.csl
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

<!--
Template skeleton for an experiment-tier slide deck. See research/slides/README.md
for the convention. Replace TITLE/Issue # and edit slides below; delete this comment.
-->

## The question {.smaller}

::: {.incremental}
- One-sentence framing of the question this experiment asks
- One-sentence framing of why it matters now (parent-Issue context, decision rule it feeds)
:::

. . .

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
Decision-rule outcome lands on slide N (results) → slide N+1 (decision).
:::

<!--
ALTERNATIVE "big-question" pattern (for tool-eval decks or when a punchier
opening reads better):

::: {style="text-align: center; font-size: 2.2em; font-weight: bold; line-height: 1.2; margin-top: 0.4em;"}
First line of the question\
second line\
third line?
:::

. . .

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**Why now:** parent-Issue context.
:::

AVOID `r-fit-text` for multi-line big-question slides. The reveal.js
`.r-fit-text` class scales each block to canvas WIDTH regardless of
content height — stacking 3 r-fit-text lines silently consumes the
entire vertical canvas and pushes any follow-up callout off the bottom
(caught in HERMES eval deck, Issue #218, 2026-05-26). Use a controlled
`font-size` in `em` units instead — it's predictable across multi-line
content and leaves explicit room for the callout-note underneath.
-->

---

## Method (block diagram) {.smaller}

```
[ INPUT 1 ]  ─┐
              ├──▶ [ TRANSFORM ] ──▶ [ EVALUATION ]
[ INPUT 2 ]  ─┘
```

- Bullet: what each block produces
- Bullet: where the gold-standard comparison happens
- Bullet: what we threshold / sweep

---

## Data scope {.smaller}

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Patient(s) | patient_001 |
| Tissue | … |
| Chromosome scope | chr22 only / full genome |
| Sample size | … |
| Reference | GRCh38 (UCSC hg38) |

---

## Universe / metric definitions

Use LaTeX freely. Example AP formula:

$$\mathrm{AP} = \sum_n (R_n - R_{n-1}) \cdot P_n$$

- Define universe explicitly (positives, negatives, total)
- Reject alternative framings with one-line rationale

---

## Headline result

| Metric | Value | Caveat |
|---|---|---|
| AP / AUC-PR | **0.XXX** | …  |
| Best F1 | **0.XXX** at τ = X.XX | … |
| Precision @ best F1 | 0.XXX | … |
| Recall @ best F1 | 0.XXX | … |
| 95% CI on F1 | [0.XXX, 0.XXX] | … |

---

## Figure: Precision-Recall

![Precision–Recall curve (AP = X.XXX)](figures/pr_curve.png){width=80%}

---

## Figure: P / R / F1 vs threshold

![P/R/F1 across τ; best F1 at τ = X.XX](figures/prf1_vs_threshold.png){width=80%}

---

## Figure: Bootstrap F1 distribution {.smaller}

![Bootstrap F1 (n=1000, F1-at-fixed-τ, positives-only)](figures/bootstrap_f1.png){width=70%}

::: {.fragment}
**CI semantics:** F1-at-fixed-τ, not CI of max-F1 over τ. See deck's methods slide.
:::

---

## The honest headline {.smaller}

::: {.incremental}
- *Which* metric is the honest one (and why; e.g. depth-confounded precision)
- *Which* metric is misleading and why
:::

---

## Decision rule

::: {.r-fit-text}
🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴
:::

::: {.fragment}
- One-sentence decision: GREEN / YELLOW / RED with caveats
- What this feeds next (next-experiment Issue # + carve-outs)
:::

---

## Caveats {.smaller}

::: {.incremental}
1. Scope (e.g. chr22 PoC, not whole genome)
2. Depth confounder
3. Single-track / single-tissue
4. Ground-truth definition limit
5. Sample size on positives bag
6. Bootstrap semantics
:::

---

## Next steps {.smaller}

- **Next experiment:** Issue #XXX (one-sentence summary)
- **Pairs with:** Issue #YYY (filter integration / scale-up)
- **Scale-up gotcha to address:** … (e.g. streaming-parquet for full-genome)

---

## References

::: {#refs}
:::

<!-- bibliography renders here, auto-populated by Pandoc from refs.bib via [@cite-keys] in the text above -->
